scriptText:
I. THE LAST BALCONY

EXT. APARTMENT ROOFTOP - DUSK

[SHOT 1: EWS - LOW ANGLE, HANDHELD]
The massive silhouette of the apartment building against a bruised sky. Two tiny figures sit on the ledge. Streaks of light (VFX) cut through the clouds.
SFX: DISTANT SIRENS AND CITY CHAOS SWELL.

ROSA (O.S.)
Looks like the courtyard.

MARK (O.S.)
The stars are falling now.

[SHOT 2: OTS MARK - MS, HANDHELD]
Mark gazes at the horizon, a faint, sad smile on his lips. He turns slowly to Rosa.

[SHOT 3: OTS ROSA - MS, HANDHELD]
Rosa’s eyes are glassy, reflecting the fire in the sky. She holds back tears.

MARK
I still owe you that dinner.

[SHOT 4: CU ROSA - EYE LEVEL, HANDHELD]
Rosa glares at him with a cynical, watery-eyed smirk.

ROSA
(Sarcastic)
Fifteen years, Mark. Right on time.

[SHOT 5: CU MARK - EYE LEVEL, HANDHELD]
Mark lets out a dry, soft laugh. He looks back at the sky.

MARK
Just watch. It’s on the house.

[SHOT 6: MCU - LOW ANGLE, HANDHELD PUSH-IN]
Mark slowly closes his eyes.
Rosa turns her head, looking at Mark with a gaze of quiet admiration and awe.
A beat.
Rosa slowly closes her eyes as well, leaning into the wind.
SFX: THE SIRENS BLEND INTO A SINGLE HIGH-FREQUENCY WHINE.

II. THE WITNESS

INT. GUEST ROOM - NIGHT

[SHOT 1: MS TO ECU - ARC, TRACKING / CONTINUOUS TAKE]
The door EXPLODES open. ROBERT (45) hauls ROSA (30) inside. He SLAMS the door shut—the frame SHAKES. He stays pinned against the wood, blocking the exit.

ROBERT
The driveway, Rosa. Again.

The camera SWINGS around Robert's shoulder to find Rosa. She’s trembling, her back hitting the cold window glass.

ROSA
(A panicked whisper)
She said... she said we didn't need her.

Robert LUNGES forward, closing the six-foot gap in a second. The camera PITCHES with him, landing in a claustrophobic profile.

ROBERT
(Overlapping)
Stop. He’s gasping for air and you’re just reciting her scripts.

ROSA
(Desperate)
It’s her house, Rob! I have nowhere—!

ROBERT
(Cutting her off, a cold snarl)
You’re not a daughter anymore. You’re a witness.

The camera DRIFTS closer, tightening on Rosa’s face. The anger in her eyes dies, replaced by a hollow, terrifying void. She looks past him at the door.

ROSA
(Voice breaking)
He won’t even fight for himself...

The camera CREEPS into an ECU of Rosa’s eyes. A tear tracks through her makeup.

ROSA
Why am I the only one dying in this house?

Robert’s reflection in the window behind her remains frozen. His voice is a low, devastating hum.

ROBERT (O.S.)
Because you’re the only one she has left to break.

The camera holds on Rosa’s shattering expression as Robert’s shadow RECEDES from the wall behind her.

III. THE LAST WEIGHT

EXT. RUINED APARTMENT COMPLEX - DUSK

[SHOT 1: WS to MS - EYE LEVEL, HANDHELD/ONER]
The camera sits wide, revealing a smoking graveyard of masonry. A small, white figure—MARK—stumbles from an ambulance into the center of the frame. The camera PUSHES IN and SHAKES, tracking him as he reaches the rubble.

MARK
(Breathless, guttural)
No. No.

He drops to his knees, clawing at a heavy slab. His bloodied fingers leave red streaks on the white dust. He stops, looking back at the SILENT CROWD of survivors. He locks eyes with ROBERT.

MARK
Help me! Do something!

Robert steps forward into the frame, his shadow falling over Mark. He places a heavy, steadying hand on Mark’s trembling shoulder.

ROBERT
Mark. It’s finished.

Mark’s chest hitches—a sharp, wheezing ASMA ATTACK. He looks up at Robert, his expression shattering. He looks at the silent rubble.

MARK
It’s... so quiet.

Mark’s hands go limp, sliding off the stone. He stares into the void of the debris. Robert gently pulls him away.

IV. BROKEN

INT. COLONIAL OFFICE - DAY

[SHOT 1: MS - EYE LEVEL, HANDHELD]
The camera stays on ROBERT. He stands with his back to us, looking through the wooden shutters. He is a statue in white linen. From the hallway, we hear a MUFFLED SHOUT and a SCUFFLE.

The heavy doors CRASH open (SFX: WOODEN IMPACT). MARK bursts in, chest heaving. The camera WHIPS to catch him. He is a mess of batik and plaster. He kicks a small side chair (SFX: CLATTER).

MARK
(Breathless, voice cracking)
You knew. You sat here and you knew!

The camera circles Mark as he storms toward the desk. Robert turns slowly. He is calm, which only fuels Mark’s fire.

ROBERT
Your arm, Mark. You’ll break the cast.

MARK
(Overlapping)
Three days! He was gone for three days! You let me eat... you let me...

ROBERT
(Cutting in)
I did it to protect you! You were in no state—

MARK
(Screaming)
I should have been the one!

Mark reaches Robert. His right hand grabs Robert’s gold buttons. His left arm (the cast) is pinned between them, awkward and heavy. Mark’s face is a mask of agony. He’s trying to hold back a sob, but his breath comes out in jagged shudders.

MARK
(A broken whisper)
He was my brother.

The camera moves in tight on Robert’s face. His stoic expression shatters. His eyes well up. He reaches out and pulls Mark into a crushing embrace. Robert’s hand goes to the back of Mark’s head, stroking his hair with a fatherly rhythm.

MARK
(Guttural sob)
Uuuggghhh—!

Mark’s weight fully drops into Robert. Robert’s eyes close, a single tear cutting through his composure. The camera stays close, catching the sound of heavy sobbing and the rhythmic creak of the ceiling fan.

V. BLUE HOUR

EXT. RURAL ROAD - TWILIGHT

WIDE SHOT. The wind whips SARAH’S hair across her face. She looks exhausted, her shoulders hunched against the cold. ELIAS is a static statue, staring down the infinite, empty road.

SARAH
(Voice strained, shivering)
We’ve been out here for an hour. She isn't—

[MEDIUM SHOT - OVER SARAH'S SHOULDER.]
ELIAS doesn’t move his head, but his eyes snap toward her, sharp and defensive.

ELIAS
(Cutting her off)
I said wait.

[CLOSE UP - SARAH.]
She steps into his space, invading his "grief bubble." Her expression is a painful mix of anger and heartbreak. Tears are visible but they aren't falling; they are frozen by the wind.

SARAH
Wait for what? The wind to say her name? It’s just us, Dad. Just us and this road.

[MEDIUM CLOSE UP - THE INTERACTION.]
Elias’s head finally drops. He looks small, his denim jacket hanging off his slumped shoulders.

ELIAS
(A broken whisper)
One more minute.

SARAH
(Firmly)
A minute won't bring her back.

Sarah reaches out and GRABS his hand with a firm, desperate tug. She begins to pull him toward the camera.

SARAH
Walk with me, or I’m leaving you here.

VI. THE BRIDGE

INT. SEDAN - DAY (OVERCAST/RAIN)

[SHOT 1: MEDIUM INTERIOR (ESTABLISHING)]
Mick TURNS THE KEY. The engine COUGHS—whir-whir-click—but fails to catch. The Visitor SLAMS his palm against the driver-side glass. Rain streaks his face like tears.

VISITOR
(Muffled, frantic)
Mick! Don’t take the bridge!

LEO
Mick, who is that?

[SHOT 2: CLOSE UP - MICK]
Mick’s jaw is locked. He RIPS the key back and turns it again. The starter MOTOR WHINES. He ignores the man outside entirely, eyes fixed on the dashboard.

MICK
Don’t look at him.

VISITOR (O.S.)
Mick! Look at me! Take the dirt road!

[SHOT 3: CLOSE UP - LEO]
Leo is hyperventilating.

LEO
How does he know your name?

MICK (O.S.)
Lock the door, Leo.

LEO
He’s crying! He looks just like—

[SHOT 4: MEDIUM INTERIOR / CAR EXTERIOR]
Mick BARK-SHOUTS over the sound of the failing engine. He POUNDS the steering wheel just as the V8 FINALLY ROARS TO LIFE.

MICK
I know what he looks like!

VISITOR
(Screaming)
THE BRIDGE IS OUT! Turn Around!

Mick SLAMS the car into gear. The tires CHIRP on the wet gravel.

MICK
Hold on.

LEO
Mick, wait—!

The car LUNGES forward. The Visitor’s hand SLIDES off the glass in the rain.

CUT TO BLACK.
